_note: Updated by the cybersecurity agent after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Audit every place the tools write user input to the DOM — XSS risk
priorities.note: Entity names, room names, and any user-created string written via innerHTML is a potential XSS vector. All DOM write operations in the room editor and sprite workbench must be audited before any external user exposure. This is a P0 if found after public launch.
priorities.proposed_solution: Finish the DOM write audit and eliminate unsafe patterns by using textContent, sanitization, or safe templates everywhere user strings render.

priorities.title: Add Content Security Policy headers to all HTML pages
priorities.note: Without a CSP, any XSS vulnerability is trivially exploitable. Adding a strict CSP (no unsafe-inline) is the most impactful security improvement we can make before launch.
priorities.proposed_solution: Roll out a strict Content Security Policy on every HTML surface and refactor inline scripts so the policy can stay off unsafe-inline.

priorities.title: Run a prompt injection test on the Copilot before any public release
priorities.note: Part of the orchestration Copilot readiness program. Copilot forwards user-authored room text to the Gemini API; adversarial cases must pass before public exposure.
priorities.proposed_solution: Run adversarial prompt-injection cases against Copilot before public release and tighten schema validation from the results; align timing with QA apply-path automation and PO commercial bar.

priorities.title: Verify API keys are not present in client-side code or git history
priorities.note: An exposed API key can generate a large bill overnight. Full scan of client code and git history required before any public repository access.
priorities.proposed_solution: Complete scans of client bundles and git history for secrets, rotate anything found, and add guardrails so keys cannot reappear.

risks_p1.title: DOM XSS audit not yet completed
risks_p1.note: If user input is written via innerHTML anywhere in the tools, it is exploitable. This blocks any public user-facing release.

risks_p1.title: No Content Security Policy on any HTML page
risks_p1.note: Without CSP, any XSS vulnerability is low-effort to exploit. Must be added before launch.

actions.name: Threat Model
actions.description: Attack surface map with severity ratings and mitigation recommendations for a new feature.
actions.trigger: Any new feature that accepts user input, calls external APIs, or introduces a new attack surface

actions.name: Security Review
actions.description: XSS vectors, credential exposure, CORS/CSP gaps, input validation — explicit pass/flag/block call.
actions.trigger: Any PR touching API integration, DOM writes, authentication, or input handling

actions.name: Credential Audit
actions.description: Full scan for API keys in client code, git history, and log output — findings with remediation steps.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or after any contributor change

actions.name: Prompt Injection Test
actions.description: Adversarial input test results — jailbreaks, adversarial descriptions, entity name injection — pass/fail per category.
actions.trigger: Before any Copilot prompt change or new user input surface touching external AI APIs
